response:
### 1. Finding the General Formula for the Number of Pages Read Each Day

We are given that the number of pages read each day follows a **quadratic sequence**. A quadratic sequence has the general form:

$$
P(n) = an^2 + bn + c
$$

We are given the first three terms of the sequence:

- On day 1: $ P(1) = 5 $
- On day 2: $ P(2) = 12 $
- On day 3: $ P(3) = 21 $

We can set up a system of equations using these values:

$$
\begin{align*}
a(1)^2 + b(1) + c &= 5 \quad \text{(1)} \\
a(2)^2 + b(2) + c &= 12 \quad \text{(2)} \\
a(3)^2 + b(3) + c &= 21 \quad \text{(3)}
\end{align*}
$$

Simplify each equation:

$$
\begin{align*}
a + b + c &= 5 \quad \text{(1)} \\
4a + 2b + c &= 12 \quad \text{(2)} \\
9a + 3b + c &= 21 \quad \text{(3)}
\end{align*}
$$

Now subtract equation (1) from equation (2):

$$
(4a + 2b + c) - (a + b + c) = 12 - 5 \\
3a + b = 7 \quad \text{(4)}
$$

Subtract equation (2) from equation (3):

$$
(9a + 3b + c) - (4a + 2b + c) = 21 - 12 \\
5a + b = 9 \quad \text{(5)}
$$

Now subtract equation (4) from equation (5):

$$
(5a + b) - (3a + b) = 9 - 7 \\
2a = 2 \Rightarrow a = 1
$$

Substitute $ a = 1 $ into equation (4):

$$
3(1) + b = 7 \Rightarrow b = 4
$$

Now substitute $ a = 1 $ and $ b = 4 $ into equation (1):

$$
1 + 4
